Source_Species.reason: The paper studies CYP728B70/TwCYP728B70 from Tripterygium wilfordii.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Tripterygium wilfordii is the organism throughout the paper.

Applied_Species.reason: TwCYP728B70 was tested in T. wilfordii suspension cells and in yeast (miltiradiene-producing strain and WAT11).

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The experiments used Tripterygium wilfordii suspension cells and Saccharomyces cerevisiae yeast strains.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The gene is linked to triptolide accumulation by RNAi and overexpression in Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Triptolide is explicitly described as an abietane-type diterpenoid/phenolic diterpene triepoxide.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction/discussion state triptolide has antitumor, anti-inflammatory, immunosuppressive, and potential CNS-related activities.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Results state root bark had the highest content of potentially triptolide-related metabolites, and CYP728B70 is highest in root bark where this diterpenoid is primarily found.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: CYP728B70 is explicitly identified as involved in triptolide biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Triptolide is described as an abietane-type diterpenoid derived from miltiradiene; CYP728B70 acts on this branch.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Results show it oxidizes C-18 to form dehydroabietic acid-related products, an intermediate step before triptolide.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper identifies CYP728B70 as a cytochrome P450.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: CYP728 family membership is explicit; PF00067 is the CYP450 hmm model used in Methods.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Feeding and yeast assays showed activity with miltiradiene, abieta-8,11,13-triene, miltiradienol, and dehydroabietinol.

Primary_Product.reason: Results explicitly report formation of dehydroabietic acid, miltiradienoic acid, dehydroabietinol, and miltiradienol.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results state TwCYP728B70 catalyzes consecutive oxidations at C-18 of miltiradiene or abietatriene to alcohol and acid derivatives; aldehyde is inferred but not directly observed.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Dehydroabietic acid is presented as a downstream precursor/intermediate in triptolide biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Discussion says overexpression indicates CYP728B70 activity limits triptolide biosynthesis, but 'rate-limiting/control step' is an interpretation rather than a directly proven kinetic designation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show RNAi decreased triptolide accumulation and overexpression increased triptolide by ~70%.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All listed findings are stated in Results/Discussion: candidate identification by omics/expression, yeast product formation, RNAi decrease, overexpression ~70% increase.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all described and used: network analysis, RNAi, overexpression, yeast co-expression, feeding assays, GC-MS/UPLC, and NMR for product identification.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: CYP728B70 was co-expressed with TwCPR3; WAT11 also expresses an Arabidopsis CPR.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Discussion states CYP728B70 has low transcript levels overall, highest in root bark; Results note MeJA-responsive candidate selection.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Same T. wilfordii system shows highest expression in root bark and induction under MeJA.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methyl jasmonate induction of suspension cells is central to transcript/metabolite analysis.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly mention constitutive promoters pPGK1-pTEF2 for Leu-PT replacement of GAL promoters, but pH7WG2D is a vector rather than a promoter; the exact promoter used in plant overexpression is not specified here.

Omics_Data.reason: BioProject PRJNA542587 and use of metabolomics (UPLC/Q-TOF MS) and RNA-seq are explicitly stated.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion states CYP728 family had no previously assigned function; aldehyde intermediate was not observed; similarity to conifer CYP720B is discussed.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly highlights utility for improving triptolide production and engineering yeast to produce dehydroabietic acid.
